contributor: university of illinois at urbana-champaign library, urbana, il
description: Dawn of New Day For Women of Orient By Jean Piper HE Methodist Episcopal Board of Foreign Mis sions comb their church for candidates to fill their foreign missionary posts and then sift them again to see which is the more fit one believes when you look into the unswerving great gray eyes of Herbert Welch resident Bishop of Japan and Korea on leave in this country In the intricate mazes of the strong wholesome Character of Bishop Welch there is that dash of states manship so essential to a man called to occupy the post that most delicate of all positions bishop in a foreign country Not that they over use this ability for political purposes for that is strictly against the rules but It keeps them clear of local entanglements and enables them to keep straight in the channel of the strict missionary field That scrutinizing sidelong glance the glimpse of the white bulldog teeth when he smiles are the Rooseveltian physical charactistics this man stationed in the Orient possesses For eight years he has been Bishop of Japan and Korea with headquarters at Seoul Korea He has just been reappointed for another four years One feels that
